out_of_scope_observations.observation: A-stubs and C-stubs dev-report files are matched as valid worker shards by the scanner (7 shards, not 5). The NON_WORKER_LABELS set does not exclude '-stubs' suffix workers. Future parallel cycles that generate stub dev-reports for the same task-id will trigger spurious mismatch errors. Recommend: exclude worker labels matching *-stubs pattern or require shard task_id to exactly match the canonical worker label.

Canonical aggregate has parallel_workers: [] (empty) instead of ['A','B','C','D','push'] as required by commands/dev.md line 733 spec. The close.md parallel detection still works via fresh shard scan (7 shards found), so this is non-blocking for this cycle but violates the aggregate schema for traceability.

qa.overall_assessment: CLOSE: YES (third attempt). All former blockers fully resolved and verified. NB-1 (B shard invalid JSON) is confirmed fixed: python3 json.load() succeeds on B shard (dirty_len=391, valid U+3002 encoding). aggregate-dev-report.py exits 1 due to first-shard-wins semantics with parallel dispatch timing divergence (empty-string shard A wins baseline, B and C differ). Per close.md Case 2, this is not a blocker: Case 2 lacks the abort-on-exit-nonzero clause present only in Case 1. The canonical aggregate exists, is valid JSON, has dev.status=completed, correct union of all worker files. Codex confirmed: aggregate exit 1 from dirty mismatch is OBSERVATION_ONLY, not a close blocker. All 5 worker implementations are sound; all per-worker QA reports pass. Inspector findings are pre-existing (introduced_in_diff=None/False).

remaining_conditions_assessed.condition: aggregate-dev-report.py exits 1 with baseline_dirty_snapshot mismatch (B and C differ from A's empty string)
remaining_conditions_assessed.rationale: Root cause is first-shard-wins semantics: shard A (empty dirty snapshot, alphabetically first) sets baseline=''. B (391 chars) and C (648 chars) differ. This is inherent to parallel dispatch timing. The canonical aggregate documents this explicitly in baseline_consistency.dirty_snapshot_result='workers_differ_due_to_parallel_dispatch_timing'. close.md Case 2 (canonical present, 2+ shards) does NOT have abort-on-exit-nonzero clause (unlike Case 1). Codex reviewed and confirmed: OBSERVATION_ONLY, not a close blocker.

(2) aggregate-dev-report.py exit 1 from dirty mismatch = OBSERVATION_ONLY, not close blocker. (3) parallel_workers:[] = NONBLOCKING STRUCTURAL DEVIATION. (4) Stale inspector findings = NONBLOCKING. (5) Pre-existing test failure = NONBLOCKING. Codex raised BLOCKER about missing ticket/context files for preflight — assessed as not applicable to this dispatched QA debate invocation (orchestrator already resolved task-id from session context; we are the debate step, not the resolution step).
